Payment Terms

Payment terms define when an invoice is due for payment. You set them up once and attach them to suppliers, clients, or individual invoices. Merlin calculates the due date automatically whenever an invoice is created, based on the rule and the invoice date.

What payment terms do

A payment term is a reusable rule that, given an invoice date (and optionally a billing period day), calculates a due date. Payment terms can be set as defaults on suppliers (for purchase invoices) and clients (for sales invoices), and can be overridden on any individual invoice. A default set of payment terms is seeded on installation. See Seeding — Payment terms.

Term rules

Each payment term uses one of six calculation rules:

RuleHow the due date is calculatedExtra fields used
N Days After InvoiceDue date = invoice date + N daysDays
Nth of Following MonthDue date = the Nth day of the month after the invoice monthDay of month
First Business Day of Following MonthDue date = the first working day of the month after the invoice monthNone
Same as Invoice DateDue date = invoice date (payment on delivery)None
Beginning of Next Billing PeriodDue date = the start of the next billing period, as configured by the billing period day settingNone (uses billing period day)
N Working Days Before Month EndDue date = the last day of the invoice month minus N working daysDays

Fields

FieldNotes
NameA descriptive label, e.g. "Net 30" or "EOM"
RuleOne of the six calculation rules listed above
DaysNumber of days; required for the "N Days After Invoice" and "N Working Days Before Month End" rules
Day of monthDay number (1–31); required for the "Nth of Following Month" rule

Due-date calculation

When an invoice is created or a payment term is assigned, DueDateCalculator resolves the due date from the rule, the invoice date, and (for the billing period rule) the billing_period_day setting. The calculated due date is stored on the invoice and can be overridden manually at any time.

Managing payment terms

  1. Go to Billing › Payment Terms.
  2. Click New Payment Term to add a new term, or click the edit icon on an existing one to modify it.
  3. Select the rule, fill in the required extra fields (days or day of month), give it a descriptive name, and save.

The default terms seeded during installation can be edited or supplemented as needed.

Permissions

  • payment-terms-view-any / payment-terms-view — to view payment terms
  • payment-terms-create — to create new terms
  • payment-terms-update — to edit existing terms
  • payment-terms-delete — to delete terms
  • payment-terms-restore / payment-terms-force-delete — to restore or permanently delete soft-deleted terms
